_comment: Illegal clause detection rules. blocks_generation: true = this rule blocks certification even on freshly generated docs (truly illegal content). blocks_generation: false = advisory only for generated docs, becomes blocking after user edits. context: PRESENCE = fires when bad pattern found. ABSENCE = fires when required pattern missing.

rules.message: Perpetual or absolute non-compete clause is void under ICA S.27.
rules.suggestion: Limit non-compete to a reasonable duration tied to protection of Confidential Information or goodwill.

rules.message: Absolute ouster of court jurisdiction is void under ICA S.28.
rules.suggestion: Replace with an arbitration clause specifying the seat and rules.

rules.message: Agreement contains a clause with an unlawful object — void under ICA S.23.
rules.suggestion: Remove any clause with an object that is forbidden by law, fraudulent, or opposed to public policy.

rules.message: Bonded labour clause detected — absolutely prohibited under the Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act 1976.
rules.suggestion: Remove any clause requiring work without pay or restricting freedom to leave employment.

rules.message: Clause references employment of minors in violation of Child Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act 1986.
rules.reference: Child Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act 1986 – S.3
rules.suggestion: Remove any reference to employing persons below 14 years of age.

rules.message: Discriminatory clause detected — violates Constitution Art.15 and Equal Remuneration Act 1976.
rules.reference: Constitution of India – Art.15; Equal Remuneration Act 1976 – S.4
rules.suggestion: Remove any clause that discriminates on the basis of caste, religion, sex, or gender.

rules.message: Clause purports to waive Fundamental Rights — unenforceable under Constitution Art.13.
rules.suggestion: Remove any clause requiring parties to waive their constitutional or fundamental rights.

rules.message: Penalty clause may be disproportionate — courts will award only reasonable compensation under ICA S.74.
rules.suggestion: Cap penalties at a reasonable estimate of actual loss. Indian courts will not enforce punitive liquidated damages.

rules.message: Clause allows unilateral modification without consent — creates free consent risk under ICA S.10.
rules.suggestion: Amendments should require written consent of all parties.

rules.message: Interest rate appears usurious. Courts may reduce it under the Usurious Loans Act 1918.
rules.suggestion: Keep interest rates commercially reasonable (typically below 24% per annum for commercial loans).

rules.message: Agreement may contain deferred or unresolved terms — ensure all key obligations are certain and not left for future agreement.
rules.suggestion: Replace vague terms like 'reasonable', 'fair', or 'as agreed' with specific measurable obligations.

rules.message: Clause references foreign currency payment — FEMA compliance required for cross-border transactions.
rules.reference: Foreign Exchange Management Act 1999 – S.3, S.6
rules.suggestion: Specify RBI approval requirements and FEMA compliance obligations for any foreign currency payment.

rules.message: Payment clause does not address TDS deduction — non-compliance with Income Tax Act 1961 S.194C/194J.
rules.suggestion: Add a TDS clause stating that applicable taxes will be deducted at source as per the Income Tax Act 1961.

rules.message: Clause may facilitate sharing of unpublished price-sensitive information — prohibited under SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations 2015.
rules.reference: SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations 2015 – Regulation 3
rules.suggestion: Ensure confidentiality clause covers unpublished price-sensitive information and add SEBI compliance provisions.

rules.message: Clause may constitute an anti-competitive agreement void under the Competition Act 2002 S.3.
rules.suggestion: Remove any price-fixing, market-sharing, bid-rigging or output limitation provisions which are per se void under S.3(3).

rules.message: Payment terms exceed 45 days — may violate MSMED Act 2006 if payee is a Micro or Small Enterprise.
rules.reference: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Development Act 2006 – S.15
rules.suggestion: If counterparty is an MSME, payment must be made within 45 days of acceptance. Add MSME compliance provision.
